College: Emory College of Arts and Sciences — Humanities & Languages
Description: Histories, cultures, and political movements of Black communities in the United States and the global African diaspora — cross-disciplinary inquiry combined with community engagement.
Histories, societies, and cultures of African peoples with an interdisciplinary lens. Strong study abroad and internship opportunities in Africa and London.
Patterns of American culture across history, literature, sociology, and cultural studies — region, class, race, ethnicity, and gender as analytic frames.
Interconnections of the ancient Near East, Egypt, Greece, and Rome through artifacts, ideas, and exchange. Archaeological projects and study abroad in Israel, Turkey, Greece, and Italy.
Arabic language fluency combined with Arabic-speaking cultures. Intensive summer program in Morocco and semester programs across the MENA region.
Visual analysis of painting, sculpture, photography, architecture, and performance in historical and contemporary contexts, with attention to studio art and critical theory.
Chinese language combined with Chinese literature, culture, and society. In-country study abroad is integral.
Classical Greek and Roman world through literature, art, archaeology, history, philosophy, and religion. Strong preparation for law, medicine, business, and publishing.
Languages, literature, culture, and influence of ancient Greece and Rome — direct access to foundational texts through rigorous linguistic training.
Joint major pairing classical languages/literature with English literary studies — the influence of antiquity on modern literature and culture.
Classics combined with historical methods — the ancient world through primary-language study and historiographical frameworks.
Ancient Greek and Roman texts integrated with philosophical inquiry — ethics, metaphysics, and epistemology in the classical tradition.
Literature in English and other languages — Western and non-Western works examined through philosophy, religion, and psychology, with multilingual reading.
Movement as an intellectual, emotional, and kinesthetic form of knowledge — performance, choreography, theory, and history.
Intensive Chinese, Japanese, or Korean language combined with literature and culture. Semester abroad in China, Japan, or Korea strongly encouraged.
Interdisciplinary histories, societies, and cultural production of East Asia — substantial language acquisition and regional study abroad required.
British and American literature, poetry, drama, fiction, and creative/critical writing — Shakespeare to science fiction to contemporary theory.
Literary traditions integrated with intensive creative practice in poetry, fiction, playwriting, screenwriting, and creative nonfiction.
Relationships between literature and historical context — individualized program connecting textual analysis with historiographical methods.
Media literacy and critical thinking about how images and sounds construct meaning — history, theory, and cultural context of film and media.
French language combined with French and Francophone literature, culture, and history. Six-week Paris summer immersion and semester EDUCO programs.
Literary, cultural, linguistic, and historical study of the German-speaking world with study abroad and international career preparation.
Classical Greek language enabling direct reading of literary works in the original — foundational to Western intellectual tradition.
Shared human experience across time — analytical tools for political, cultural, economic, and social change globally and locally.

Majors: History and Art History (BA, Joint Major)
Description: Broad historical study combined with the analysis of art objects from Egyptian funerary artifacts to modern sculpture in political and cultural context.
Studio art practice combined with art history and theory — must be paired with another major. Study abroad and summer internships encouraged.

Majors: Interdisciplinary Studies in Society and Culture (BA)
Description: Self-designed major for topics that cross disciplinary frameworks — customized coursework, independent research, and a senior project under faculty mentorship.
Italy through literary, cultural, historical, artistic, philosophical, and political lenses, combining language acquisition with humanistic traditions.
Japanese language from elementary through advanced levels combined with electives in literature, gender and sexuality, and cultural studies.
Jews and Judaism through history, religion, linguistics, literature, theology, political science, and anthropology — foundation for graduate or organizational careers.
Classical Latin enabling direct reading of literary works of ancient Rome with increasing fluency and depth.
History and contemporary life of Latin America and the Caribbean — coursework taught in English and Spanish across history, social sciences, politics, arts, and literature.
Language across physiology, cognition, meaning, society, and culture — critical thinking about language structure and linguistic research.
History, theory, and critical analysis of media forms and institutions — print journalism to digital platforms in social, political, and cultural context. Distinct from Film and Media.

Majors: Middle Eastern and South Asian Studies (BA)
Description: Languages (Arabic, Hebrew, Hindi, Persian, Tibetan) combined with history, the arts, and comparative culture across the Middle East and South Asia.
Music across three tracks — Composition, Performance, and Research (ethnomusicology, music history, music theory) — with 60+ faculty artists.
History of philosophy combined with ethics, metaphysics, aesthetics, and epistemology — core liberal arts training in critical inquiry.
Philosophical inquiry into ethics, meaning, and human existence integrated with the academic study of religion.
Justice, democracy, rights, and political legitimacy at the intersection of philosophy and politics — strong preparation for law school and public policy.
Theater studies and creative writing combined — playwrights as writers and theater professionals working through concept to production with established artists.
Intellectual study of the world's living religions — comparative and thematic exploration of religion in public life, gender, and culture.
Academic study of religion combined with anthropological inquiry — religious practices embedded in and shaping human social life.

Majors: Religion and Classical Civilization (BA, Joint Major)
Description: How religion shaped foundational ideas in the classical world — ancient literature, art, history, and philosophy with comparative religious study.
Religion and the human experience through historical methods — logically structured exploration of religion in historical change.
Academic study of religion combined with sociological analysis of human social behavior, groups, and institutions.
Intensive Russian (or related language) acquisition combined with geography, politics, history, and film of the former USSR and Eastern Europe.
Languages, texts, and cultures of the Spanish-speaking world — Spain, Latin America, and the United States — with critical engagement across literary, film, and media texts.
Spanish language acquisition combined with theoretical linguistics — how Spanish and related languages develop, change, and signify meaning.
Spanish and Portuguese combined — literature, linguistics, theater, music, and translation across the Ibero-American world.
Acting, theater history, criticism, design, and technical theater — Atlanta's arts scene providing internship and career opportunities.
Gender and sexuality through an intersectional lens that considers socioeconomic status, race, and national identity — critical thinking, research, and creative expression.
The Lusophone world — Portugal, Brazil, Angola, Mozambique, Cape Verde — through language, literature, culture, and history with strong study abroad encouragement.

College: Emory College of Arts and Sciences — Social Sciences
Description: Holistic study of human beings as biological and cultural organisms across the four subdisciplines: biological anthropology, archaeology, linguistics, and cultural anthropology.
Interaction between biology and culture with particular relevance for pre-med students — anthropological perspectives on health and human biology.
Production, distribution, and consumption of goods and services — strong theoretical and empirical training in microeconomics and macroeconomics.

Majors: Economics and Computer Science (BA, Joint Major)
Description: Economic reasoning, empirical methods, and data analytics combined with machine learning and AI — economic phenomena in the digital age.

Majors: Economics and Human Health (BA, Joint Major)
Description: Economics paired with pre-health preparation for students interested in health economics, health policy, medicine, veterinary science, and related fields.
Mathematics-intensive economics preparation for doctoral economics programs or quantitatively oriented MBA programs.
Scientific, social, historical, and cultural dimensions of health and well-being — preparation for professional/graduate schools and careers in health policy, global health, and health research.
International relations and comparative politics — social-science foundation with a political science core and global scope.
Systematic study of government, politics, and political behavior at local, national, and international levels. Internships and study abroad encouraged.
Human behavior and mental processes — experimental, psychobiological, social/personality/clinical, and cognitive/developmental psychology with extensive research opportunities.
How people acquire and use language — cognitive psychology combined with formal linguistics for graduate study in cognitive science or communication.
Human social behavior, groups, and interactions — the relationship between personal experience and broader social forces.

College: Emory College of Arts and Sciences — Natural Sciences & Mathematics
Description: Mathematical methods applied across science, engineering, business, and computer science — preparation for engineering, technology, finance, or applied mathematics graduate study.

Majors: Applied Mathematics and Statistics (BS, Joint Major)
Description: Statistical analysis combined with quantitative research methods — preparation for data-oriented careers, statistics or actuarial graduate school.
Fundamental biological concepts applied in the laboratory and field — foundational for pre-medical and other pre-health curricula.
Bridges biology and physics — mathematical and physical analysis of how living organisms survive, adapt, and grow from molecules to populations.
The 'central science' at the intersection of biology, physics, geology, and environmental science — preparation for medical, dental, pharmacy, and other professional schools.
Computing concepts, algorithm design, and general programming principles — preparation for software engineering, research, and technology careers.

Interdisciplinary data science combined with a substantive field across natural sciences, social sciences, or humanities. Formerly Quantitative Sciences; renamed Fall 2025. 20 interdisciplinary tracks.

Prepares students for engineering-adjacent professions (engineering management, patent law) through mathematics, chemistry, and physics. Tracks in engineering physics, materials science, or geosciences.
Ecological and social foundations of environmental issues — classroom learning, field study, internships, research, and study abroad.
Science of space and number — analytical thinking and problem-solving for liberal arts and quantitative fields.

Majors: Mathematics and Computer Science (BS, Joint Major)
Description: Mathematical foundations combined with algorithm analysis and computer science principles — rigorous mathematical reasoning paired with technical computing.

Majors: Mathematics and Political Science (BA, Joint Major)
Description: Mathematical problem-solving and analytic skills combined with systematic study of government and political behavior.
Synthesis of neuroscience and behavioral biology drawing on biology, psychology, and anthropology — evolution, animal behavior, developmental biology, systems neuroscience, and ethics.
Energy, matter, and their interactions — available for physics majors and pre-engineering students, with BA and BS tracks of varying rigor.
Physics combined with astronomy — preparation across medicine, law, graduate study, and astrophysical research.
Physics designed for life-sciences and medical-profession students — core physical principles in a biological context.

Data science rigor combined with public policy emphasis — co-offered by Data and Decision Sciences and Political Science. Preparation for quantitatively demanding government, education, or nonprofit careers.

College: Emory College — Engineering Dual Degree & Medical Imaging
Majors: Engineering (BS/BA + BSE, Georgia Tech Dual Degree)
Description: Five-to-six-year dual-degree program awarding both an Emory BA or BS and a Bachelor of Science in Engineering from Georgia Tech — full liberal arts education combined with professional engineering training.

Bachelor of Medical Science preparing students for advanced practice, management, and educational roles in medical imaging (CT, MRI) — clinical specialization, equipment application specialist roles, or imaging management.

Majors: Two-Year Liberal Arts (AA, into Emory College / Goizueta / Nursing)
Description: Two-year liberal arts program on Emory's original Oxford, GA campus — Discovery Seminars, community-engaged learning, global learning, student research, applied arts, and internships. Students complete General Education Requirements and pre-major coursework, then transfer to Atlanta as juniors to complete a full major through Emory College, Goizueta, or Nursing.

Functional area depth: financial reporting, the legal environment, taxation, ethics, and international accounting standards. Strong preparation for public accounting, corporate finance, consulting, and investment banking; pairs with the BBA/MPA dual degree for CPA requirements.

Functional area depth: analytical training for corporate finance, investment banking, portfolio management, venture capital, private equity, and commercial lending. 4+1 BBA/Master of Finance accelerated dual degree available.

Majors: BBA — Information Systems and Operations Management (ISOM)
Description: Functional area depth combining technology management and operational systems thinking — consulting, analytics, supply chain management, and technology strategy.

Functional area depth in analyzing customers and markets, creating marketing strategies, and implementing programs — popular for general management, media, entertainment, and consulting careers as well as marketing.

Functional area depth (Organization & Management) — economics, political science, psychology, and sociology applied to organizational behavior. Preparation for management consulting, strategy, and organizational leadership.
Interdisciplinary depth at the intersection of artificial intelligence and business strategy — AI for financial modeling, agent-based systems, and responsible AI deployment.
Interdisciplinary depth in development and application of analytics tools — coding, A/B testing, regression, machine learning, and AI. 4+1 BBA/MSBA dual degree available.
Interdisciplinary depth integrating economic performance with social and corporate values — ethical decision-making in complex business environments.
Interdisciplinary depth in client consulting across industries and functions — project management, communications, problem structuring, hypothesis development, and data storytelling.

Interdisciplinary depth in launching new ventures — ideation, customer discovery, financial modeling, and venture capital, with access to the Roberto C. Goizueta Center for Entrepreneurship and Innovation.
Interdisciplinary depth in global commerce — global strategies, international financial reporting, global trade and finance, and cross-border marketing. Study abroad at 45 Goizueta partner schools.
Interdisciplinary depth in real estate — market factors, properties as an asset class, development, REITs, and capital markets. Leverages Atlanta's real estate market for access and networking.
Collaborative concentration with Emory College Art History, Music, and Theater and Dance — administrative and management careers in the performing arts.

Majors: BBA — Environment and Sustainability Management Concentration
Description: Collaborative concentration with Emory College environmental science majors — history, politics, and practice of environmental management with business skills in strategy, marketing, finance, and operations.

Majors: BBA — Film and Media Management Concentration
Description: Collaborative concentration with Emory College Film and Media majors — historical and contemporary context plus business infrastructure of content creation and distribution.
Collaborative concentration with Emory College's Center for the Study of Human Health — personalized medicine, global health applications, nutrition, and health policy.

College: Nell Hodgson Woodruff School of Nursing
Description: Rigorous clinical and classroom preparation for professional registered nursing — practice in clinical and classroom settings with domestic and international service-learning. Graduates qualify for the NCLEX-RN and pursue careers in acute care, community health, global health, clinical research, and health policy. Center for Data Science access to AI/ML and 2.7 billion de-identified health records; Lillian Carter Center connects students to global service-learning partnerships.
